question: Which external callers receive bundled skills or contract guidance, and what compatibility rule binds each caller to a Tally contract version?
context: Public Ledger operations must remain caller-neutral. Codex, Claude Code, direct owner use, and Hermes may consume the same versioned command registry without private storage or provider-specific fields.
decision: The executable exposes one provider-neutral versioned operation registry and schemas. Codex and Claude Code may receive version-matched convenience skills; Hermes consumes the same released contract as an external orchestrator. Every caller fails compatibility before financial mutation and no caller-specific transport behavior enters LEDGER semantics.
